spec_match.rationale: Codemod that rewrites the seven Chrome-deprecated Intl.Locale info getters (`.calendars`, `.weekInfo`, etc.) to the corresponding method form only when the receiver is traced to `new Intl.Locale(...)`.

interactive.rationale: Source textarea + sample-loader + rewrite + findings list + copy-to-clipboard. Six controls, real codemod behaviour.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'paste any source file; rewrites each legacy getter into the new method form, lists every hit, and lets you copy the patched output.' Delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Masks strings/comments, records variables initialized from `new Intl.Locale(...)` plus simple aliases, rewrites only those typed receivers, and lists skipped untyped lookalikes such as the sample's `obj.calendars[0]`.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5148228059398144 and the TC39 PR that landed the rename.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only; .ok / .dirty findings cells use accent colours.

openQuestions.title: Show a diff view instead of two textareas
openQuestions.detail: A line-by-line diff (red strike-through on the old form, green underline on the new form) would let visitors verify the codemod's hits without manual scanning. The findings list is already populated with line + name — drive a diff renderer from that.

openQuestions.title: Bundle as a npm-installable codemod
openQuestions.detail: The page would be much more useful if it also shipped as `npx @paul/intl-locale-info-codemod ./src` for use in CI. Linking to such a package (and providing the AST-based version of the regex from #1) would let teams adopt this without manual paste.

summary: The codemod now avoids the broad regex false positive by tracing Intl.Locale bindings and simple aliases before rewriting, reports skipped lookalikes, and aligns its property list with the matrix sibling.
